sentences.text: Hey, Matt.
Hey, how's it going?
Good, how are you?
Pretty good.
This is my last meeting of the day, and then just some catch-up stuff, and then the weekend, so….
Nice!
Yeah.
Exciting.
are… does NRP have access to Explore Basic currently?
I believe so.
Exactly.
Let me double check.
I can as well, actually.
And then from a renewal perspective, when are these guys up?
And they got another year.
They signed a 3-year contract.
Sorry about that, okay.
Yes, they do have, BASIC enabled, and they have, research reports enabled as well.

… Basically, they… we've been giving them to research to them for whoever they've been asking for access, just because it wasn't available when they first signed their agreement, so it wasn't built in.
Yeah, so….
Okay, I remember that.
Yeah, this one's definitely gonna need to have a little bit of, renegotiating done on it, if they agree to add additional product, or if we can get them to just, like.
early renew with new terms, new pricing, and everything, that would probably be best, because we can't just keep throwing, like, free features at them just because they sign long-term.
Yeah, this one will be, I mean, like, Explorer for sure, Explorer Premium, it's like, this will be an additional cost.
Yeah.
And then we can rework things with them, for sure.
Will is just, he's always so price sensitive, so….
Yeah, oh, for sure.
We'll see if….
I saw his email, he said he's running late.
Oh, okay.
How late?
He said a few minutes.
Okay, we'll just hang out.
Just a couple minutes late, some, prior meetings running late, so….
Oh, good, I thought that might happen.
Yeah.
What'd be a plan for your weekend?
It's my son's birthday today.
Oh, fun!
Yeah, he turned 9.
Wow.
So, yeah, my mom heart is a little, teary-eyed today, you know.
Not 10 yet.
Not 10 yet, but he walked to school by himself yesterday for the first time, and I was not thinking that he'd be ready for that yet, so I thought I'd have, like, at least another year.
Yeah.
And that wasn't the case.
And so, he walked yesterday, he walked today, I'm like, come on, it's your birthday, let me drive you.
Like, at this point, almost begging him to let me drive you.
hike.
I just want to walk, like….
Yeah, oh….
Yeah, so….
What's he into?
Are you doing, like, a birthday, like, theme or anything?
No, I'm not doing birthday parties anymore.
We're taking him, he invited a couple of friends, and I'm taking them to an immersive game box tomorrow.
Oh, cool.
Yeah, because he's into, like, gaming, VR, all that stuff.
Yeah.
Is it, like, the sandbox?
Like, the Sandbox VR, or something like that?
Yeah, kind of similar.
It's like, they don't have, like, VR goggles on, but they, like, have this, like, camera hat on, and their body basically moves their character.
Yeah.
And the game's, like, just all on the walls around you.
So, I'm actually excited.
I might join in myself with the kids, so….
Never wanted to do one of those.

Yeah, so it's… he's excited, and then today I'm picking him up from school and taking him to this, like, trading card game store, and letting him pick out, like, whatever Pokemon sets he wants, because he's still really into those.
Yeah, I've heard that… I've heard that's back.
Oh, it's been back.
Yeah.
The binder that this kid has, I have never seen so many cards in my life.
And you're buying them for them, too.
Yeah.
So, hey, well, he's a… he's high-functioning autistic, so, like, when he gets into something, like, he knows all the HP and damage and values of these cards, and, like.
Yeah.

he started going to the… asking me to take him to the trading card game stores, and, like, just trying to trade up and sell some high-value cards, and, like, you can't have this one, like, this one's too precious, and, like.
you're kind of a nerd, but… Yeah, we're all gonna be foolishly mistaken when he's a millionaire off these years.
Well, that's what I'm telling him.
I'm like, Alex, don't sell these cards.
Save them.
They'll be worth more when you're older.
Don't make our mistakes, so…
And we're taking him to hibachi today, because that's his favorite thing for dinner, so….
Onion Volcano?
Yeah, Onion Volcano, they got little choo-choo train, whistles they'll do, like, push it down the side.
You know?
So….
fun.
Yeah.
Will, if we're wondering what we're talking about, it's not her husband.
Her 9-year-old has a birthday.
Yes.
Yes.
And is also very into Pokemon cards.
Yeah, it's awesome.
Once upon a time, I had them too.
They're no longer with me, and I'm probably not a millionaire because of it, but….
That was our mistake.
I feel like I've got binders somewhere in my childhood home.
I don't know where, though.
Yeah, it's, it's a big thing.
Like, there's so many of them now.
Back when we were younger, there was, like, a hundred.
Now there's, like, thousands.
Yeah, makes sense.
How'd you been, Will?
I've been good, yeah.
How about your… how about yourself?
Good, I moved to Atlanta, it's probably the biggest update since we last met, so….
You were in Chicago last, right?
I was, I was.
So, I got… I earned this green wall through the move, which is awesome.
Other than that, business is usual for us, so….
Oh, that's awesome.

Yeah, I've been excited with all the different updates and stuff, and I know every so often we had just done that revenue management demo, not any position to move anytime soon, but it was awesome to see, and excited about all the stuff, at least.
They keep updating these coming, so….
Yeah, and if you spend much time in… I know you guys do a ton of development, have you spent much time in the Explore module to date?
A lot of time, yeah.
Okay, I think you're probably gonna really like this, then.
I know you reached out directly on forecasting.
That's… what we do now is we've got this LinkedIn team that effectively posts from Alex's LinkedIn.
So we start to.
That's where I saw it, so….

Yeah, we start to tease what's coming out, as we're developing it, so I do have something to show you there today, but, I think if it works for you, some pretty major updates to explore just in general, so….
Good.

I don't know how much background we gave you, but, essentially what that tool is, is just the pricing and availability, same methodology that IQ has always used to collect it, now layered in with, ACS census data, some other things in there, like, Labor of Bureau Statistics, then also government, databases and building permits.
So city building permits.
And then, most recently, we partnered with Real Capital Analytics, if you're familiar with them all.
So… The data in there is pretty much fully shored up, … product will continue to grow, but I can show you current state of that, if that makes sense.
Yeah, no, that makes sense.
Cool.
Let's talk to Lion.
It was good timing, too, with all this being posted with the budgeting season.

We're, like, right in it, and we were just kind of putting our projections out to the rest of NRP's teams with, like, market rent growth, and I think everyone goes through the same spiel every year, where it's like, well, we'll blend this one, we'll blend that one.
it's a really tough game to give people any good information, so that's kind of where my head was at when I saw this, and I was like, wow, perfect.
And I'm assuming… are you just using, just using CoStar, and then, some RealPage products?
CoStar, RealPage, you already blended, you know, everyone has a different projection, nobody's right, but….
Yeah.
How, what's, like, how far off are, like, what's the delta on those?
I'm sure it varies by, by market, and….
Yeah, that's the… that's the difficult answer.
You kind of just answer yourself, but it's… it's so different by all these markets.

I just feel like it's… it's even more difficult, it feels like, now than it was even 5 years ago, just because it's so much… there's been so much pent-up supply that's, you know, hitting, and everyone's kind of trying to guess what the next year looks like.
I feel like it's stabilizing.
Yep.
Finally, but….
I don't know, I… there's been a lot of misses.
That's what we've heard, and I guess when you all look at this, like, what's the ideal, geography for you?
Is it, like, a sub-market level, market level, MSA level?
How do you….
you get most accurate, obviously, once you just start having these big market-level things, but it doesn't mean a lot to me, because you have to be able to get down to some kind of sub-market level.
I love that within the Explore piece, you can essentially draw out your areas.

I think that's huge, like, if we want to carve out areas… I know I pulled the… the development team before, even, like, within certain school districts, sometimes that can be really impactful in more of our suburban markets.
But just being able to get down reasonably to the sub-market level, so we're looking more at our comp set area versus these really expansive areas.
Makes sense.
Full transparency, forecasting's not there yet, so he started with the… and just background for you, we brought on, I believe his name's Mike Taylor.
He was the, one of the head economists over at CoStar for a really long time, so he's the one that's, leading this product development.
And effectively, what we did is gave him all the historical data that IQ has, trend data, and then access to all the new databases we have as well.

So, probably pretty similar methodology, but if you think about who's putting out forecasts, and if you think about the level of accuracy that the data in IQ has, we think that we'll be a very competitive solution in this.
And I will say, either way, even if you're not there yet, I do have confidence you guys will get there from everything you've done to date.
Yeah.
But it's so hard to get this information.
There's not an easy system to get it to, so that's part of the headbanging against the wall, where it's like, we're going to all these different places, and all these little reports, even.
big companies like RealPage put out.
To get what is pretty desired information.
Right.
… Yeah.
Awesome.
So, we, can show you what it looks like today, and then I guess when you all think about this, when you're checking in on forecasting, is this like a once-a-year thing, quarterly?
How often are you looking at those numbers?
At least for the purpose of what I was just talking about, it's for our budgets.
So, I mean, that's more of a once-a-year kind of thing, but, I mean, everyone constantly wants to know what it even looks like any bit in the future.
Showing any picture, I think, is helpful.
I, truthfully, talking more to the equity partners, like, in our deals, I offer little versus too much, just because they'll hold us to it, and truthfully, we don't know.
The market keeps changing, you know?
Under-promise, over-deliver, right?
That's right, yeah.
Awesome.

Okay, well, just, just for your knowledge there as well, we're updating these number, like, we're updating the forecast monthly, is the current, pace at which we're doing it, but that's liable to, liable to change.
And then, just reminding, I guess, like, how many markets at a given time are you all keeping your eyes on?
I mean, it's really just the markets we're operating in, slash trying to develop in.
I mean, Texas, we're huge, and a lot of the Florida markets, DC… Now, more Jersey, New York.
Okay.
Some of Boston, not much, truthfully.
Trying to get more into the Phoenix and… Nevada markets… So not….
Understood.
In 10-ish or so, maybe.
Yeah, so the guidance of that is just, we know these markets pretty well, and we're very in touch, we know where we'd want to develop, essentially, with how we develop.
I think it's also just where the opportunities, at least our development teams see are.
I know there's a couple other areas, like, look, we're in Ohio, we're based out of Cleveland, but there's just some markets that we're redder operated in, I guess.
Okay.
Interested in that, just because we hear different investment theses, obviously, and one of the tools I'll show you today is actually a market comparison tool.
So, … ability to track, I believe right now it's an MSA level, a county level, and a city level, across basically all the data points that we'll review and explore, with trend data tied to it.
So if you're sourcing 20, 30 markets for the right opportunity at once.
Higher, higher level top of funnel to kind of source where opportunities might be.
Hey, and I know I said that to… I'm blanking on his name right now.
He's… With your development team.
Mitch, potentially?
Yeah, Mitch, I'll send him things every once in a while, but that's what I told him for the research reports.

I was like, look, there's… we're only just starting to breach into different parts of our organization using that, but I know even with what's out there, they're excited about it, so I think the more… things that come up like that, I think they would absolutely love to see.
Awesome.
Just different, maybe, customer bases, but….
Yep.
I'll give you the run-through, and then, obviously be happy to set time if we're like, this is the place we want to have that conversation.

So, you'll see that this… I think we kind of talked about this before, the direction that we're going is to just start to eliminate logins for everybody that's using a lot of different softwares that are providing data.
So… Revenue management is now within the tool, if you're using it.
Won't provide any updates there today, happy to meet on that again, but… Explore tool here, you see, looks pretty different.

I think last time we met, we kind of talked about Explore Basic as its current function would kind of roll into this Market Summary tab, and that's done, so… What you have here is the components that existed, or currently exist in Explorer Basic, with some new visualizations, and obviously the added element of forecasting.
So… Rent performance lives here.
Again, this is at an MSA level right now, but we are forecasting out, 5 years.
And again, that's just using our historical data and then current Trends on, on supply and demographics, and it's updated on a monthly basis.
30-day market snapshot, which you're familiar with.
Historical data back 5 years at this point.
Availability, leasing, and occupancy back, 5 years as well.
Right-hand side here, should look pretty similar.
… Black Dots, existing inventory, Yellow triangles under construction.
Directly out of here, you're gonna be able to create a, a research report.
So, newer since the last time we met is, additional filtering options.
So, I think property class here is new.
We did bring in ownership information to the platform, so the ability to filter by that.
Can look at existing or out of construction inventory.
And then, started to pull in some performance metrics, rent metrics that you're able to filter by here, as well, in addition to the previous filters, which were amenities, stories, and floor plan.
So again, your development team knows this is what we developed, here's the amenities on it, here's the unit counts, …
That's gonna reduce this down, and you've got this new property list here.
So, and….
One question, maybe, not to cut you off here, but.
Oh, God.

with the construction pieces, I know even, like, within using it today, I feel like it's helpful as far as knowing things that I would assume are more coming, but as far as the actual dates go, do you guys feel like those are stale?
Do you feel like they're accurate?
That's my biggest challenge, as it's getting closer.
It's almost… I get the question a lot, and truthfully, I have no idea for some of these.
Yeah, it is.

So, the thing with this inventory right now, it's updated monthly, but you have that, like, the scheduled construction is the bucket that's furthest off, because you might have something… this means a permit has been accepted.
the build hasn't started.
And so, there's obviously a portion of this unit that.
these units that may actually never go through.
And so there's a bit of staleness there.
We do think that, the partnership with Real Capital Analytics will shore some of that data up and make it more accurate.

So, I think it's like with, like, with anything with IQ, what you're getting right away… so, for instance, this delivery by quarter, we're actually gonna backfill this with historical as well, so you can see how supply has changed, or if new… if more building is starting to occur in that market.
And I'm sure we'll be working on the accuracy of the data as well.
Yeah, because that's the piece that… I think as it gets closer, that's more, it's like, there's pieces I do know today, like, and I've seen, I'm like, this is still stale.
And I don't….
more just, like, how I would use it, like, if I had pretty, pretty strong confidence all of the deliveries were going to happen, I would just pull up the chart on a call.
Yeah.
But I don't today.
I use it internally for myself.
I guess, like, what would build more trust there?
Is it just gonna take time of you matching one-to-one?
I think it's, like, me checking delivery dates I know are gonna happen, and having them at least be close.
Yeah, but how far….
So you're talking about, like, this delivery date?
Yeah.
Gotcha.
So, ideally for you, this is like a month and a year, and then it actually delivers that.
Honestly, I'm talking about stuff that's really close, too.
So, if it's August now, we know they're starting hard hat tours, we know that they're really close.
Next 3-4 months, right?
Got it.
And it might… it almost looks like it's just information that was once upon a time, right, maybe?
But not fully….
Yeah, it's government databases and… yeah, government databases and building permits, so, right, that's… yeah.
What… have you found a source that, you feel to be closest on that?
Going website to website and calling them.
Okay, so you're hoping we figure it out.
Yeah, it's… it's honestly, it's… I mean, it's never easy with that, but look, like, I'm only doing that for, you know, sites that we are actively trying to lease up.
We're not doing it for perspective.

I think that could be awesome for… the perspective piece for more of the development side, but for us, it's not that big of a lift when we're competing against, you know, maybe one or two that's coming online versus what's already active, and we can track an apartment IQ.
Since they're gone.
Okay, makes sense.
It's kind of a one-time lift, too, or a check-in.
Yep, it's good feedback.
Great feedback.
So, … I can follow up on that, I'll certainly send that through to Megan and Mitch.
This is actually new, this is great.
See, this is so fun, Will, we just built something, I haven't even seen this yet.
Me and Explorer are still getting to know each other a little bit.
So I was gonna say, the ability to export this will come soon, but I guess, at least in the beta version, it's there.
And you can see over here, like, how I've got them sorted.
By property class, high to low.
But let me show you the new… if I can get back to where I was going… I've been going to this guy.

So, I think you know this, but, … Apartment IKEA is, like, a sister company of a parent company, Rentable, which has had ILS for, like, 15 years, and so, you'll see some elements now, like pictures and many pictures within, the platform.
So, within the property pages, where formally, it's like you're just sourcing the, one picture of the property.
for the purpose of showing the data underneath, we'll actually start to bring in some visualizations and explore.
That's nice, too, because you can kind of stay within the tool, and actually, as you're researching, hey, is it a comp, is it not?
Like, have something for the interiors.
And then, like you said, right here, like, there's observed metrics that we're pulling out, and there's calculated metrics, and if you ever need to cross-check the data, you got that link right here.
But you'll see it's a brand new look.
So you got… you have your building specs here, management, ownership group, and, last sale price.
So, any transaction data.
Not in the tool yet, but coming will be sales comps, as well, as you're talking transactions.
But, you'll see here, for each individual property, we're going to give you a one-year performance trend.
So, as you click into a property in Explorer, we're going to auto-generate a set of 10 comps, much like we do in research right now.
And so we're going to give you access to that trend data right away without, you know, even looking at the comp set.
Subject property, blue line, comp green line, market red.
So, current state of this is, your rent buckets, Exposure, occupancy, or lease percentage.
Concession values or percentages.
And then lease activity.
I always like to call out here, this number is trailing 30 days, so that's, like, what the data points are being updated on a monthly basis here.
That's good, too, because I feel like so much, you get all the fall-off out of the way of what actually happened or not.
That's why I love when you guys have the applications and the actual leases split.
It's almost like you can feel what gross is, but then you know what's real.
Yep.
you have to have it delayed to know that.
There's no other way, I feel like.
Yep, exactly, and it's like anything with IQ, maybe you, at some point, see a data frequency here that you'd be able to toggle on and off, but, current state, monthly data.
Looking here at pricing availability, I've got a 30-day window, but maybe I go 90.
And same thing that you can see in floor plans and bed type right now, you're getting a net effective rent trend on each of the bed types.
And then you have your, … Floor plan buckets here as well.
Like, anything that's gonna be clicked into, so if you want to look at those actual units, you're able to do that.
as much as I love all this, I will say, too, I feel like everything you're showing me is actually even more conducive for more of the upfront side, it feels like.
Yep.

Which… I… look, I feel like he was already excited about the research reports and all that stuff, too, but, I feel like that is a helpful way to be able to kind of also figure things out as they're going and researching, and….
Yeah, because one of the things I'll… and I'll… if you feel like this is more relevant for that side, happy to meet with them.

One of the things I'll show you here is just, you got this estimated rent roll, and some new metrics, like your loss to lease percentages, your average lease length, resident tenure, etc, so… That side of the table.
And then, for that research side.
Directly from here, you can generate that research report.
So, I think you probably see the picture, but, yeah, everything you need to know about the property.
How do they… how did the… how does that loss to lease part work?
We're gonna learn that together, I think.
So….
It's almost just like if they think we're undervalued, or… It can't be your existing rent, you know, because I don't know how you guys would know that, but….
Yeah, let's see what we got here… between market rent and leased rent divided by lease rent.
Market rent and leased rent.
I would think a lot of the times it would be pretty similar, unless, I guess, if you're looking in the past, like, the more historical….
I'll have to check… I'll have to check on that.
We do have, again, at 5 years of historical data, and this is over, … I mean, I would assume that's taken into account.
I don't assume that's taking into account the historical data.
Yeah, I mean, look, if you already had the data of what at least that one year goes by, then you'd still have it.
I mean, unless they moved out or something, but…
Yeah, since we have this in the revenue management software as well, so I'm assuming it's the same logic that we're using across both.
Isn't it?
No, I mean, cool either way.
I just… yeah, I mean, it's like, how… I was thinking, I was like, how the heck did you get that?
Yeah.
I can follow up with you on that as well.
That's good.

Alright, so that's Explore, and then I'll just quickly show you this, and then… I know you're primarily interested in forecasting, which is still in beta, so I won't be able to get you access to that, but, this is looking interesting.
We're happy to meet with whoever on your team, so… Concept here, any, MSA, county, city.
You can apply your investment thesis here, so whatever you're typically looking at, if you want to compare apples to apples across markets.
Right, and this would hold into Explorer as well.
And then.
Can you save these?
Yeah.
That's… yeah, that's pretty cool.

That's where I feel like that's what I… I want a lot and explore, and I know I told Mitch that, too, because I was like, I feel like half the time, I just want… I wish it was almost on the market survey tab, as, like, I could save it and say, I want a feel of the market before even looking as deep as the comps always.
That makes sense.
Yeah, this will be saveable.
One of the other things that doesn't have anything to do with what we're talking about today is portfolio-level reporting, which is coming soon as well.
So I think maybe you've requested that in the past, but that should be live in a couple of weeks, or you can pull everything out, basically, at once.
Yeah, that's cool, just to see performance across your own portfolio versus the comps, generally.
Yep, exactly.
Yeah, supply here, so all those data points we were just talking about rolled in, and then you've got all your trend data alongside of it.
And then, again, you got 20 markets, one of them looks good.
Click in, it's gonna pull those filters over.
You have your forecasting the market, and you're inventorying the market, and directly from there, you can start to generate, so….

It is interesting, it makes me think, like, when I look at that market comparison tool, just… I would think, like, more intrinsically, if you're a developer, you kind of generally know where you want to go, but it maybe reaffirms, like, if you had every sub-market in the city on a thing, and certain metrics are popping for you, maybe… You focus more, ….
Yeah, that was MSA level.
You can go down to… I mean, you can pull each… so, I just did an example in Dallas, so you have every zip code in Dallas, right?
And see what's jumping out to you.
Yeah, no, that's cool from that side, for sure.
Yeah.
That's all that's new.
So, I don't know, happy to, happy to meet with the upfront side on this.
Happy to keep you updated as, as, forecasting develops further.
So….
Yeah, I mean, I'm always… I'm interested as the forecasting develops sooner.
I feel like I'm still interested within, like… that side's pretty neat.
I feel like it would be probably worth, … Scott, who I think you guys have met Scott before.
Yeah, yeah, that's….
God is really the upfront piece.
Like, I work with him some, but he is the end-all of the upfront, so he works directly with the developers.

But, yeah, it might not be a bad idea sometime after Labor Day that if we even have some time to show him that, too, because I've been trying to draw him excitement around using it anyways, because I think he does use a lot of what I'd say is a little bit more of the older tools, that it might just hyperdrive things for him.
Yeah, happy to do that.
I'll just get in your inbox, you can forward it through to him, or connect us at some point.
And then, I don't know if you have 3 or 4 MSAs, MSA levels that you're looking at forecasting for.
Send them over, I'll screenshot them.
Yeah.
Fire them your way, and then you can tell us how good the data looks compared to what else you're seeing.
Yeah, no, for sure, absolutely, we'll compare it.
Kind of a fun starting point, and then we'll let you know as we get more granular with that.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Awesome.
Well, enjoy your long weekend, always good catching up.
Yeah, no, absolutely, I appreciate you walking me through this and showing me, so… You can tell them good marketing, too, that was spot-on marketing.
Yeah, yeah, he was, ….
We're in the face with it.
Recently, he picked a fight with, who's that found?
Yeah.
Yeah, Reba?
Yeah, so we're trying to have a little bit of fun with it.
the LinkedIn comments.
Sure.
Yeah, we're… yeah, we're figuring that side of things out.
Yeah.
The tool is actually… we'll have to set another walkthrough of that in a couple months.
Really, we really shored up the explainable AI component of it.
And we now have all of our lease data in there.
So we've got, like, 16 million leases that's informing.
First, I think LRO was, like, 5 million when they got going, so….
Yeah, it's a lot.
You know what I should ask yet while I got you guys?
I know that Mitch said this was going to be sometime near the end of summer, but that there was some enhancements coming to the research report side for the sorting piece.
I know that was the side I always pinged.
Yeah, ….
Is there any update on that, roadmap-wise, or…?
If you don't know….
We do have now the ability to do this within Market Survey.
So you can search directly here.
I've seen that, yeah.
And same thing in research.
We don't think.

where you can currently, like, sort through your created research reports, which is, I think, what you guys were looking at because of the different, like, … statuses, I think there were within your research reports, or ….
Yeah, and it's really, like, different users, right?
So it was, like, what I had sent him… was, truthfully, not helping myself for a future contract, but, like, there's multiple users and different reasons they'd use it, right?
Like, from more of the property management, like, it's getting built, we have to compare versus comps, like, there's the operational, like.
